Control Arm.evidence: In the 'METHODS' section on page 1, it states: 'Patients with mCSPC (N = 1,052) were randomly assigned 1:1 to receive apalutamide (240 mg QD) or placebo plus ADT.' This indicates that 'placebo plus ADT' is the control arm.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: On page 1, under 'METHODS', it states: 'Patients with mCSPC (N = 1,052) were randomly assigned 1:1 to receive apalutamide (240 mg QD) or placebo plus ADT.' This indicates two treatment arms: one experimental arm (apalutamide plus ADT) and one control arm (placebo plus ADT).

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The 'PURPOSE' and 'METHODS' sections on page 1 state that patients were assigned to receive 'apalutamide (240 mg QD) or placebo plus ADT'. The 'INTRODUCTION' section on page 1 further clarifies that 'apalutamide' is an 'androgen signaling inhibitor'.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 4, in the 'RESULTS' section under 'Study Population', it states: 'Patient disposition from the intent-to-treat population (N = 1,052 [apalutamide plus ADT, n = 525; placebo plus ADT, n = 527]) is summarized in the Data Supplement.' The control arm is 'placebo plus ADT', which had n = 527 participants.

Treatment Arm(s).value: apalutamide plus ADT; placebo plus ADT
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: "Patients with mCSPC (N = 1,052) were randomly assigned 1:1 to receive apalutamide (240 mg QD) or placebo plus ADT." This indicates the two treatment arms of the study.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section of the abstract, it states: 'Patients with mCSPC (N = 1,052) were randomly assigned 1:1 to receive apalutamide (240 mg QD) or placebo plus ADT.' This is further confirmed on page 4, in the 'Study Population' section under 'RESULTS', which states: 'Patient disposition from the intent-to-treat population (N = 1,052 [apalutamide plus ADT, n = 525; placebo plus ADT, n = 527])'.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'Patients with mCSPC (N = 1,052) were randomly assigned 1:1 to receive apalutamide (240 mg QD) or placebo plus ADT.' This indicates that Treatment Arm 1 received apalutamide (240 mg QD) plus ADT.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The value '44.0 months' is explicitly stated in the 'RESULTS' section on page 1: 'With a median follow-up of 44.0 months, 405 OS events had occurred...' It is also mentioned on page 5, in the first paragraph of the right column: '...the median follow-up time was 44.0 months.'

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'The median treatment duration was 39.3 (apalutamide)'. This is confirmed on page 7 in the 'Safety' section and in Table 3 on page 8 under 'Apalutamide Plus ADT'.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'The median treatment duration was ... 20.2 (placebo)'. This is confirmed on page 7 in the 'Safety' section and in Table 3 on page 8 under 'Placebo Plus ADT'.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 4, in the 'Study Population' section, it states: 'Patient disposition from the intent-to-treat population (N = 1,052 [apalutamide plus ADT, n = 525; placebo plus ADT, n = 527])'. This indicates 525 participants in the apalutamide plus ADT (experimental) arm. This is further supported by Table 1 on page 3, which lists 'Apalutamide Plus ADT (n = 525)' under the ITT Population.

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study, 'TITAN', is explicitly mentioned in the title of the article: 'Apalutamide in Patients With Metastatic Castration-Sensitive Prostate Cancer: Final Survival Analysis of the Randomized, Double-Blind, Phase III TITAN Study'. It is also consistently referred to throughout the abstract, methods, and conclusion sections, and listed under 'CLINICAL TRIAL INFORMATION' on page 9.

Quality of Life Scale.value: Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (FACT-P) questionnaire
Quality of Life Scale.evidence: On page 2, in the 'End Points' section, it states: 'Patient-reported HRQoL was assessed using the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (FACT-P) questionnaire.' This directly identifies the scale used to measure quality of life.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in Figure 1C, under 'Subgroup' -> 'Disease volume' -> 'High' row, and 'Apalutamide' column, the value is 134/325.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in Figure 1C, under 'Subgroup' -> 'Disease volume' -> 'High' row, and 'Placebo' column, the value is 175/335.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in Figure 1C, under 'Subgroup' -> 'Disease volume' -> 'Low' row, and 'Placebo' column, the value is 60/192.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'Health-related quality of life, per total Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate, in both groups was maintained through the study.' The 'CONCLUSION' section also mentions 'maintained health-related quality of life'. On page 2, the 'End Points' section states: 'Patient-reported HRQoL was assessed using the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (FACT-P) questionnaire.' On page 7, under 'Patient-Reported Outcomes', it explicitly discusses the 'Analysis of change from baseline in the total FACT-P score showed that favorable baseline HRQoL per total FACT-P was maintained with the addition of apalutamide to ADT, with no substantial between-group differences (Fig 3).'

NCT.evidence: The NCT identifier is found on page 9 under 'CLINICAL TRIAL INFORMATION' as 'NCT02489318 (TITAN)'.

PubMed ID.evidence: The document does not explicitly report a PubMed ID.

Trial Name.evidence: The trial is referred to as 'Phase III TITAN Study' in the main title on page 1 and consistently as 'TITAN study' throughout the document.

Author.evidence: The first author listed on page 1 is 'Kim N. Chi, MD'.

Year.evidence: The publication year '2021' is stated on page 1 in the journal citation 'J Clin Oncol 39:2294-2303. 2021' and also under 'Accepted on March 23, 2021 and published at ascopubs.org/journal/jco on April 29, 2021'.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is a multi-page article with detailed sections including methods, results, and discussion, indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: The title on page 1 explicitly states 'Phase III TITAN Study'. The PURPOSE section also mentions 'phase III, randomized, placebo-controlled TITAN study'.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The title 'Final Survival Analysis' and the statement in the PURPOSE section 'Herein, we report final efficacy and safety results after unblinding and placebo-to-apalutamide crossover' on page 1 indicate this is a follow-up analysis of the TITAN study.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: The document provides age categories in Figure 1C on page 4 (e.g., < 65, 65-74, >= 75 years) and the number of patients within these categories for both apalutamide and placebo groups, but it does not explicitly state the overall median age in years for the treatment arm.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: The document provides age categories in Figure 1C on page 4 (e.g., < 65, 65-74, >= 75 years) and the number of patients within these categories for both apalutamide and placebo groups, but it does not explicitly state the overall median age in years for the control arm.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The document mentions "regardless of whether patients had metastases at primary diagnosis" on page 2 and presents a "Metastasis stage at diagnosis" subgroup in Figure 1C on page 4, which includes M1 (metastatic at diagnosis), indicating reporting by synchronous metastases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The document does not explicitly report results based on prognostic groups of patients with metachronous diseases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The document explicitly states on page 6 that the treatment effect was favorable across prespecified subgroups, "including... with both high-volume and low-volume disease (definition of disease volume per CHAARTED12)". Figure 1C on page 4 also shows "Disease volume" as a subgroup with a "High" category.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The document explicitly states on page 6 that the treatment effect was favorable across prespecified subgroups, "including... with both high-volume and low-volume disease (definition of disease volume per CHAARTED12)". Figure 1C on page 4 also shows "Disease volume" as a subgroup with a "Low" category.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: Overall Survival (OS) and radiographic Progression-Free Survival (rPFS)
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 2, under the 'METHODS' section, in the 'End Points' subsection, it explicitly states: 'The dual primary end points were OS and rPFS.' It then provides definitions for both.

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Figure 1C, under "Gleason score at baseline", row "≤ 7", column "Apalutamide", reported as "48/174". Calculated percentage as (48/174)*100 = 27.59%, rounded to 27.6%.

Gleason score - N (%) | ≥ 8 | Control.evidence: Found in Figure 1C, under "Gleason score at baseline", row "> 7" (which corresponds to ≥ 8), column "Placebo", reported as "172/358". Calculated percentage as (172/358)*100 = 48.04%, rounded to 48.0%.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: Time to initiation of cytotoxic chemotherapy, time to pain progression, time to chronic opioid use, time to skeletal-related event, time to symptomatic local progression, time to PSA progression, PFS2 (second progression-free survival), and time to castration resistance.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 2, under the 'METHODS' section, 'End Points' subsection, it states: 'Secondary end points were time to initiation of cytotoxic chemotherapy, time to pain progression, time to chronic opioid use, and time to skeletal-related event. Other clinically relevant end points were time to symptomatic local progression, time to PSA progression based on Prostate Cancer Clinical Trials Working Group 2 (PCWG2),10 and PFS2. Time to castration resistance was assessed in an ad hoc analysis.' These are also summarized in Table 2 'Secondary and Other Efficacy End Points' on page 6.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: The document states the study population consists of patients with metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (mCSPC), implying all patients had metastases. However, it does not explicitly differentiate between synchronous (metastases at the time of diagnosis of prostate cancer) and metachronous (metastases that occur after the diagnosis of prostate cancer) metastases for the treatment arm. The 'Metastasis stage at diagnosis' subgroup in Figure 1C does not provide this specific breakdown, and the numbers for M0 and M1 do not sum up to the total patients in each arm, making it unsuitable for this extraction.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The abstract states, 'apalutamide significantly improved overall survival (OS) and radiographic progression-free survival in patients with metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (mCSPC) receiving ongoing androgen deprivation therapy (ADT).' This indicates that apalutamide is given in combination with ADT, making it the add-on treatment.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a primary research paper (a randomized controlled trial report) and does not provide an 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT)'. This type of judgment is typically found in systematic reviews or meta-analyses that assess the quality or risk of bias of multiple studies, not in a single study report itself.

Treatment Class.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) with an Androgen Signaling Inhibitor (Apalutamide)
Treatment Class.evidence: The abstract and methods sections state that patients received 'apalutamide' or 'placebo plus ADT'. The introduction further clarifies that outcomes are improved with the addition of an 'androgen signaling inhibitor' or docetaxel to 'standard androgen deprivation therapy (ADT)'. Apalutamide is an androgen signaling inhibitor used in combination with ADT.

Type of Therapy.value: apalutamide (240 mg QD) or placebo plus ADT
Type of Therapy.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'Patients with mCSPC (N = 1,052) were randomly assigned 1:1 to receive apalutamide (240 mg QD) or placebo plus ADT.' This describes the treatment provided to participants.

Table 3 provides 'event rate/100 patient-years of exposure' for 'Any TEAE of interest' Grade 3-4 (103 (7.6) for Apalutamide Plus ADT), but not the N (%) as requested for all-cause Grade 3 or higher adverse events in the main document.

The 'DISCUSSION' section on page 8 mentions 'The most common treatment-related AEs related to treatment with apalutamide were rash and fatigue (Data Supplement)', indicating detailed data is in the Data Supplement.

The document does not provide a summary N (%) for treatment-related adverse events with a grade of 3 or higher in the control arm. The 'DISCUSSION' section on page 8 mentions 'The most common treatment-related AEs related to treatment with apalutamide were rash and fatigue (Data Supplement)', indicating detailed data is in the Data Supplement.

In the 'DISCUSSION' section on page 8, the last paragraph states: 'no new safety signals were detected, and no treatment-related deaths occurred.' Grade 5 adverse events are typically deaths, and this statement applies to the overall study, including the treatment arm.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: The document reports 'Prior docetaxel use' as a baseline characteristic (58 patients in the apalutamide arm, Figure 1C, page 4) and 'Docetaxel' as a first life-prolonging subsequent therapy (37 patients, 26.8% of 138 patients who discontinued due to progressive disease, Table 1, page 3). A single, combined number and percentage for docetaxel administration 'before or during the trial' for the entire treatment arm is not explicitly reported in the document.
